blockingIssues.description: Relay server hard-binds to 127.0.0.1 (src/relay/server.ts:128), preventing external/container ingress required for hosted relay operation.
Feature description requires persistence bootstrap but relay entrypoint (src/relay/index.ts:14) only loads config and starts HTTP server with no persistence initialization path.
Logout does not re-gate protected command execution (VAL-AUTH-005 gap). CLI command dispatch checks init but not auth session, so protected commands work after logout.
Status flow does not validate persisted token liveness (VAL-AUTH-006 gap). Expired/revoked tokens are reported as 'authenticated' with no recovery UX in status/protected paths.
Relay production entrypoint does not provide tokenVerifier or participantStore to createRelayServer, so auth guards are not actually enforced in default runtime.
Auth and participant checks are conditional/fail-open (src/relay/server.ts:124). When verifier/store are absent, protected routes return 200 instead of 401/403, violating VAL-AUTH-003/004.
mors init does not call generateDeviceKeys/persistDeviceKeys (src/init.ts:133), so per-device E2EE key provisioning is not satisfied by the runtime bootstrap path.
Bootstrap guard helpers exist but are not integrated into any CLI/relay secure messaging path, so secure messaging commands are not actually blocked before bootstrap completion.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Relay auth/authz dependencies MUST fail closed. When tokenVerifier or participantStore are not provided, all protected routes must return 401/403 — never fall through to 200. No legacy/backward-compatible fail-open modes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: auth-relay-guards-and-object-authorization implemented conditional guards (if tokenVerifier) that silently skip auth when dependency is absent, creating fail-open behavior. This ambiguity allowed the worker to choose legacy mode over strict enforcement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Relay server listen address MUST default to 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) for hosted/containerized deployment. Use MORS_RELAY_HOST env var to override for local development (127.0.0.1).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: relay-service-runtime-scaffold hard-bound to 127.0.0.1, blocking container/hosted ingress. Feature description says 'hosted relay' but no explicit binding guidance existed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify mors-global-worker skill step 8 (manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks): make manual checks conditional on whether the feature exposes user-facing CLI/API surfaces. Pure library/primitive features that only add internal modules + unit tests should not require manual surface checks. Workers should set followedProcedure=false when skipping required steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 4 of 5 features in relay-foundation had workers mark followedProcedure=true while only reporting automated commandsRun with no manual/interactive checks. The skill requires manual checks but the features were library-level primitives without exposed CLI/API commands.

rejectedObservations.observation: tsx not declared in devDependencies, making relay:dev non-deterministic
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking code issue already captured in relay-service-runtime-scaffold review. The relay:dev script is a development convenience and tsx availability is implied by the existing npm ecosystem; not worth a library/services.yaml update at this stage.
